_verification_note: Numbers transcribed from a representative IEC 60364-5-52 Annex B reference. Cross-checked against BS 7671 Appendix 4 mV/A/m values (which imply equivalent loop R): 1.5mm² Cu XLPE BS-7671 mVAm=29 → conductor R=14.5 Ω/km at the BS-tabulated reference temperature, which matches this file's PVC@70°C value rather than XLPE@90°C. The thermal-correction ratio R(90°C)/R(70°C) = (1+0.00393×70)/(1+0.00393×50) = 1.065 confirms 15.4/14.5 = 1.062, so the brief's PVC/XLPE numerical split is internally consistent. BS 7671 Table 4D2B appears to tabulate XLPE at the PVC reference temperature — a known BS/IEC harmonisation difference. Recommend a second-source pair check against the published IEC 60364-5-52:2009+A1:2011 Annex B PDF before downstream skills rely on tight tolerances.
_title: IEC 60364-5-52 Annex B — Cable AC Resistance and Reactance per Unit Length
_reference: IEC 60364-5-52:2009+A1:2011 Annex B, Tables B.52.1 to B.52.4. PVC and XLPE insulated cables; conductor temperature 70°C (PVC) / 90°C (XLPE). 50 Hz frequency. Stranded class-2 conductors per IEC 60228.
_purpose: Source of truth for calc.iec60909_cascade and calc.voltage_drop on IEC/BS jurisdictions. Runtime engine reads {csa × material × installation_method × insulation} → (R per km, X per km).
_temperature_correction: Tabulated R values are at 70°C (PVC) / 90°C (XLPE). For other operating temperatures, apply R(θ) = R_table × (1 + α × (θ - θ_table)) where α = 0.00393 K⁻¹ Cu, 0.00403 K⁻¹ Al.

_units.r_per_km_ohm: ohms per kilometre at tabulated temperature (50 Hz AC, stranded class-2)
_units.x_per_km_ohm_touching: reactance for trefoil/touching arrangement (typical multicore + sheath-contact singles)
_units.x_per_km_ohm_spaced: reactance for spaced arrangement (singles laid flat or vertical, one diameter apart)

_x_method_factor_note: Engine should use installation_method to select the X column. For the simple cascade case (touching trefoil or sheath-contact), use 'x_per_km_ohm_touching'. For free-air singles with spacing ≥1×diameter, use 'x_per_km_ohm_spaced'.

copper_pvc_70c._reference: IEC 60364-5-52 Annex B Table B.52.1 — Cu/PVC, 70°C conductor
copper_pvc_70c._installation_methods: A1/A2/B1/B2 (in conduit) and C (clipped direct) — R column applies to all. X column varies by trefoil-touching vs spaced.

copper_xlpe_90c._reference: IEC 60364-5-52 Annex B Table B.52.2 — Cu/XLPE, 90°C conductor
copper_xlpe_90c._note: R values ~6.5% higher than 70°C PVC due to operating temperature (1+α×Δθ = 1+0.00393×20 = 1.079; tabulated stranded-class-2 with AC skin/proximity effect ≈1.062-1.065). X values identical to PVC (geometric).

aluminium_pvc_70c._reference: IEC 60364-5-52 Annex B Table B.52.3 — Al/PVC, 70°C conductor
aluminium_pvc_70c._note: Aluminium R approximately 1.64× copper at same CSA (ρ_Al/ρ_Cu ≈ 1.64). X values identical to copper (geometric). Aluminium below 16mm² not generally tabulated — use copper instead.

aluminium_xlpe_90c._reference: IEC 60364-5-52 Annex B Table B.52.4 — Al/XLPE, 90°C conductor
aluminium_xlpe_90c._note: Aluminium XLPE — combined uplift for both material and temperature (Al base × 1.065 thermal).

_consuming_skills: calc.iec60909_cascade (downstream of source impedance in cascade path)
calc.voltage_drop (replaces the prose 'X ≈ 0.07-0.10 mΩ/m' approximation in part5-52-voltage-drop.json with per-CSA values)
_known_discrepancies: BS 7671 Table 4D2B XLPE mV/A/m values, when converted to conductor R, match this file's PVC@70°C column rather than XLPE@90°C column. BS appears to tabulate XLPE at PVC-equivalent reference temperature. Use this IEC file directly for IEC jurisdictions; cross-check before applying BS values in engine flow for tight tolerances.
